message:
Refactor CLI rendering to use OutputContext and fix atomic label assignment in quick-create

Migrate all rich terminal rendering in audit, changelog, count, stale, and sync
commands from direct Console::default().print_renderable() calls to ctx.render()
and ctx.newline(). This routes all output through the OutputContext abstraction,
ensuring consistent behavior across output modes (rich, plain, JSON, quiet) and
eliminating scattered Console instances.

Fix the quick-create command (q) to validate and assign labels atomically during
Issue construction rather than adding them in a separate post-creation loop via
storage.add_label(). This prevents partial label assignment on storage errors and
simplifies the control flow.

- src/cli/commands/audit.rs: ctx.render() instead of console.print_renderable()
- src/cli/commands/changelog.rs: ctx.render() instead of console.print_renderable()
- src/cli/commands/count.rs: ctx.render() instead of console.print_renderable()
- src/cli/commands/stale.rs: Remove Console::default(), use ctx.render()/ctx.newline()
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs: ctx.render()/ctx.newline() in flush, errors, import, status
- src/cli/commands/q.rs: Move label validation before Issue construction, assign directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
